vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on motor mount due to cooling inefficiency.
risk.mit: Conduct CFD simulations to optimize cooling inlet design.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient structural integrity at spar attachment flange.
risk.mit: Perform stress analysis to validate structural design.

risk.desc: Limited accessibility through service port may hinder maintenance.
risk.mit: Review service port design for improved access.

risk.desc: Threaded connections may not meet engagement depth requirements.
risk.mit: Add bosses or inserts to ensure proper thread engagement.

risk.desc: Fastener patterns may not align optimally with load directions.
risk.mit: Realign bolt patterns to better match load vectors.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

physics_paths.description: Cooling air flow from motor pod to cooling inlet.
Structural loads from wing spar to equipment bay door.
Human access for equipment maintenance.

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate spar attachment flange with wing spar for structural integrity.
Ensure service port provides easy access to equipment bay.
Optimize cooling inlet shape for efficient airflow.

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: visual inspection for surface finish, dimensional checks for critical features

generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Structural integrity under lift, landing, and payload loads.
Continuous load path from wing skin to fuselage frames.
Thermal dissipation from electronics to wing surface.
Human/service access for maintenance and battery replacement.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to complex mating surfaces.
risk.mit: Implement additional alignment aids and conduct thorough pre-assembly checks.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion affecting load path continuity over long flights.
risk.mit: Include thermal expansion joints and regular maintenance checks.

risk.desc: Difficulty in maintaining precise tolerances during FDM manufacturing.
risk.mit: Use advanced FDM printers with high precision capabilities.

risk.desc: Possibility of damage to service cutouts during routine maintenance.
risk.mit: Design service cutouts with reinforced edges and provide protective covers.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient preload on bolted joints leading to loosening over time.
risk.mit: Conduct periodic torque checks and re-tighten as necessary.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.description: Lift and landing loads transfer from wing skin to main spar via ribs and carry-through to fuselage frames.
Heat generated from flight electronics dissipates through the spar to the wing surface.
Access for maintenance and battery replacement through dedicated service cutouts.

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate sensor modules into sensor pockets.
Provide easy access for battery replacement through service cutouts.
Route electrical harnesses through harness interfaces.

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings

verification_focus.description: Ensure the final mass is within the budget of 1500g.
Verify that all structural members are connected and form a continuous load path.
Check that all service cutouts and interfaces are accessible and properly dimensioned.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to snap-fit interface.
risk.mit: Implement alignment guides and ensure proper assembly order.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal stress on wing ribs due to uneven heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Optimize rib design for uniform heat distribution and use thermally conductive materials.

risk.desc: Structural failure risk if control surface cutout is oversized.
risk.mit: Review and validate the size of the control surface cutout to ensure structural integrity.

risk.desc: Possibility of improper fastener preload leading to joint failure.
risk.mit: Specify torque values for fasteners and provide training on proper assembly techniques.

risk.desc: Risk of aerodynamic inefficiency due to inaccurate airfoil section.
risk.mit: Conduct CFD analysis to validate the accuracy of the airfoil section design.
